@@ -490,6 +490,7 @@
 {"id":"brenner_bot-ivx","title":"Fix artifact merge: ignore payload id/kill fields","description":"## Problem
The artifact merge algorithm currently allows delta payloads to overwrite system-owned fields like `id` and kill metadata (e.g. `killed`, `killed_by`, `killed_at`, `kill_reason`).

### Why this matters
Per `specs/artifact_delta_spec_v0.1.md`, IDs are assigned by the merge for `ADD` operations ("target_id MUST be null (ID will be assigned by merge)"). Allowing a payload to set or mutate `id` breaks determinism + referential integrity and can create collisions or confusing artifacts.
Similarly, kill fields should only be mutated by the `KILL` operation (audit trail / semantics).

## Root cause
In `apps/web/src/lib/artifact-merge.ts`:
- `applyAdd` builds new items as `{ id: newId, ...payload }`, so payload can override `id`.
- Research thread creation does `{ id: "RT", ..., ...payload }`, so payload can override `id`.
- `applyEdit` loops over payload keys and will write `id` / kill fields directly onto existing items.

## Fix
- In `ADD`: explicitly ignore/strip `id` and kill fields from payload; always set `id` after payload.
- In `EDIT`: ignore attempts to edit `id` or kill metadata.
- Add focused unit tests proving payload cannot override `id` (ADD + EDIT + research_thread creation).

## Done when
- New tests pass and prevent regressions.
- `apps/web` unit tests + build pass.

 {"id":"brenner_bot-iyo","title":"Contact Web of Stories for transcript permission","description":"# Goal
Contact Web of Stories to request permission to host the Sydney Brenner transcript text.

Status: This is a human task (requires sending an email and tracking the reply).

---

# Why
We have a working policy decision that full transcripts should not be publicly hosted without explicit permission.

---

# Email template (copy/paste)
Subject: Permission request to host Sydney Brenner Web of Stories transcript text

Hello Web of Stories team,

I’m building a research project called “Brenner Bot” that studies the scientific method of Sydney Brenner using the Web of Stories interview transcripts.

I’d like to ask for permission to host the transcript text for the Sydney Brenner interview on a website (brennerbot.org) and in a public Git repository, with clear attribution and links back to the original videos/pages.

The purpose is educational and research-oriented: to build tools that help people (and AI assistants) learn and apply Brenner’s approach to hypothesis formation and discriminative experiment design.

If you’re open to this, could you please confirm:
1) Whether we may host the full transcript text publicly.
2) Any required attribution language, backlinks, or copyright notices.
3) Any restrictions (non-commercial only, excerpts only, etc.).

If full hosting is not permitted, we can restrict to short excerpts/quotes and link back to Web of Stories.

Thank you for your time, and thank you for preserving these interviews.

Best,
<YOUR NAME>
<OPTIONAL CONTACT>

---

# Acceptance
- Permission response received with a clear go/no-go decision (and any conditions).

 {"id":"brenner_bot-j90h","title":"Testing Infrastructure Epic: Full Coverage Without Mocks","description":"# Testing Infrastructure Epic

## Goal
Achieve comprehensive test coverage using real implementations instead of mocks, with detailed logging throughout.

## Current State
- 95%+ code coverage but API routes heavily mocked
- E2E has excellent logging infrastructure
- CLI tests use real subprocess execution (no mocks)
- Core library tests follow "NO mocks" philosophy

## Gaps Identified
1. **API Routes**: Mock agentMail, auth, next/headers
2. **Multi-Agent Orchestration**: Only dry-run tested
3. **Real-time/WebSocket**: No tests
4. **Persistence Layer**: In-memory only
5. **Component Tests**: Several missing (jargon, operators, evidence pages)

## Success Criteria
- All API route tests use test fixtures or real test servers
- E2E covers full session lifecycle with real Agent Mail
- Detailed structured logging in all test suites
- No test relies on mocked business logic

## Dependencies
- brenner_bot-ukzy (audit work informs test gaps)
- brenner_bot-17wm (artifact-merge audit complete)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-01T20:57:08.59244998-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T18:45:08.691126121-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T18:45:08.691126121-05:00","close_reason":"All blockers closed (hd7m,k1zv,ua47,ukvr,e7u9,tqjb,kgb1,ai7o); testing infra foundation complete","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-j90h","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ukvr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T09:11:54.152963633-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-j90h","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-hd7m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T09:11:54.207755177-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-j90h","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-tqjb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T09:11:54.240269058-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-j90h","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-e7u9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T09:11:54.273679295-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-j90h","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ua47","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T09:11:54.305841705-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-j90h","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-k1zv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T09:11:54.337738596-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-j90h","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-kgb1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T09:11:54.369466008-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-j90h","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-ai7o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T09:12:18.356209556-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-jabd","title":"Task: Session Lifecycle E2E Tests","description":"# Goal
Create E2E tests for the complete session lifecycle from creation to artifact viewing.

# User Journey to Test

## Session Creation Flow
1. Navigate to /sessions/new
2. Authenticate (if required)
3. Enter research question
4. Select excerpts from corpus
5. Choose Brenner operators (optional)
6. Configure session options
7. Submit session
8. See confirmation/redirect

## Session Monitoring Flow
1. View session list
2. Select active session
3. See status updates
4. View agent contributions
5. See progress indicators
6. Handle session completion

## Artifact Viewing Flow
1. Navigate to completed session
2. View compiled artifact
3. See hypothesis slate
4. See test designs
5. See adversarial critiques
6. Export/share artifact

# Test File Structure
```typescript
// e2e/session-lifecycle.spec.ts
import { test, expect } from './utils/test-fixtures';

test.describe('Session Lifecycle', () => {
  test.beforeEach(async ({ page }) => {
    // Ensure authenticated state
  });
  
  test('creates new session with research question', async ({ page, logger }) => {
    await navigateTo(page, logger, '/sessions/new');
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Enter research question', async () => {
      await page.getByLabel(/research question/i).fill(
        'Does role-separated prompting improve artifact quality?'
      );
    });
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Select excerpts', async () => {
      await page.getByRole('button', { name: /select excerpts/i }).click();
      await page.getByText('§103').click();
      await page.getByText('§105').click();
      await page.getByRole('button', { name: /confirm/i }).click();
      
      logger.debug('Selected excerpts: §103, §105');
    });
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Submit session', async () => {
      await page.getByRole('button', { name: /start session/i }).click();
    });
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Verify redirect to session view', async () => {
      await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/sessions\/[a-z0-9-]+/);
      logger.info('Session created, redirected to session view');
    });
  });
  
  test('views session status and agent contributions', async ({ page, logger }) => {
    // Navigate to existing session (use fixture or create in beforeEach)
    await navigateTo(page, logger, '/sessions/test-session-id');
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Verify status display', async () => {
      await expect(page.getByText(/status/i)).toBeVisible();
      await expect(page.getByText(/(active|pending|complete)/i)).toBeVisible();
    });
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Check for agent contributions', async () => {
      const contributions = page.locator('[data-testid="agent-contribution"]');
      const count = await contributions.count();
      logger.info(`Found ${count} agent contributions`);
    });
  });
  
  test('views completed artifact with all sections', async ({ page, logger }) => {
    await navigateTo(page, logger, '/sessions/completed-session-id/artifact');
    
    await withStep(logger, page, 'Verify artifact sections', async () => {
      await expect(page.getByText(/research thread/i)).toBeVisible();
      await expect(page.getByText(/hypothesis slate/i)).toBeVisible();
      await expect(page.getByText(/discriminative tests/i)).toBeVisible();
      await expect(page.getByText(/adversarial critique/i)).toBeVisible();
      
      logger.info('All artifact sections present');
    });
  });
});
```

# Test Data Requirements
- Need fixture for completed session with valid artifact
- Need fixture for active session with partial contributions
- Need authenticated user state

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session creation flow tested end-to-end
- [ ] Form validation tested
- [ ] Session status monitoring tested
- [ ] Artifact viewing tested
- [ ] All required auth flows handled
- [ ] Loading states verified
- [ ] Error states handled gracefully","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenSnow","created_at":"2025-12-30T17:52:31.666347021-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:46:28.186447146-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:46:28.186447146-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented comprehensive E2E tests for session lifecycle: 20 tests covering session list, detail, artifact viewing, and navigation flows. All tests pass on Chrome/Firefox.","labels":["testing e2e sessions critical"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-jabd","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-hvi7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:36:46.519034241-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","title":"EPIC: Research Intelligence System (Hypothesis Lifecycle + Method Feedback)","description":"# EPIC: Research Intelligence System

## The Gap This Fills

The Brenner method is fundamentally about **killing hypotheses** — treating them as "mistresses to be discarded once the pleasure is over" (§229). The system produces artifacts with hypothesis slates, discriminative tests, assumption ledgers, anomaly registers, and adversarial critiques. But there's no infrastructure for:

1. **Tracking hypothesis lifecycle** — A hypothesis born in Session 1 might be refined in Session 3 and killed in Session 7. Currently, hypotheses exist only as disconnected items in artifact files.

2. **Tracking assumptions** — Scale checks and background assumptions are critical but not first-class entities. When an assumption is falsified, which hypotheses are affected?

3. **Tracking anomalies** — Paradoxes and surprising observations drive the method (§103: "Both could be wrong"), but they exist only as text.

4. **Tracking critiques** — The Adversarial Critic role produces attacks, but there's no way to track which critiques have been addressed.

5. **Closing the research loop** — Tests are designed but not formally connected to their execution or results.

6. **Method feedback** — The evaluation_rubric_v0.1.md defines 14 scoring criteria, but there's no implementation.

## The Six Registries

This epic builds **four first-class registries** that map to artifact schema sections:

### 1. Hypothesis Registry
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 2: Hypothesis Slate
- Stable IDs (H-xxx), lifecycle states, cross-session linking
- Fields: statement, mechanism, confidence, category, anchors, linkedAssumptions, linkedAnomalies

### 2. Assumption Registry (NEW)
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 5: Assumption Ledger
- Types: background, methodological, boundary, scale_physics
- States: unchecked → challenged → verified/falsified
- **Key**: Every program MUST have at least one scale_physics assumption (§58: imprisoned imagination)
- Falsification cascades to dependent hypotheses and tests

### 3. Anomaly Registry (NEW)
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 6: Anomaly Register
- Quarantine status: active → resolved/deferred/paradigm_shifting
- Conflict tracking: which hypotheses/assumptions does this challenge?
- **Key**: Anomalies can SPAWN hypotheses (paradox grounding)

### 4. Critique Registry (NEW)
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 7: Adversarial Critique
- Target types: hypothesis, test, assumption, framing
- Response workflow: active → addressed/dismissed/accepted
- **Key**: Unaddressed critiques weaken targets; dashboard shows "hypotheses under attack"

### 5. Prediction Registry (via Test Binding)
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 3: Predictions Table
- First-class predictions separate from tests
- Per-hypothesis expected outcomes

### 6. Test Registry (via Test Binding)
- Maps to Artifact Schema Section 4: Discriminative Tests
- **MANDATORY**: Potency checks (distinguishes "no effect" from "assay failed")
- Evidence-per-week scoring (4 dimensions, 0-12 total)
- Test→Prediction→Hypothesis binding

## Scorecard: 14-Criterion Evaluation

The Brenner Method Scorecard aligns with evaluation_rubric_v0.1.md:

**Universal (all roles):**
1. Structural Correctness (0-3)
2. Citation Compliance (0-3)
3. Rationale Quality (0-3)

**Hypothesis Generator (Codex):**
4. Level Separation (0-3, weight 1.5)
5. Third Alternative Presence (0-3, weight 2.0)
6. Paradox Exploitation (0-2, optional)

**Test Designer (Opus):**
7. Discriminative Power (0-3, weight 2.0)
8. Potency Check Sufficiency (0-3, weight 2.0)
9. Object Transposition Considered (0-2, optional)
10. Score Calibration Honesty (0-2)

**Adversarial Critic (Gemini):**
11. Scale Check Rigor (0-3, weight 1.5)
12. Anomaly Quarantine Discipline (0-3, weight 1.5)
13. Theory Kill Justification (0-3, when KILL, weight 1.5)
14. Real Third Alternative (0-3, weight 1.5)

## Research Program Dashboard

Aggregates all registries into program-level view:
- Hypothesis funnel (by state and origin type)
- Assumption health (scale checks, falsifications)
- Anomaly status (unresolved puzzles)
- Critique pressure (hypotheses under attack)
- Test execution (potency coverage, evidence scores)
- Timeline of all major events

## Feature Dependency Structure

```
Hypothesis Registry (foundation) ──────────────────┐
        ↓                                          │
Hypothesis Lifecycle States ───────────────────────┤
        ↓                                          │
Test→Hypothesis Binding (with Predictions)         │
        ↓                                          │
Assumption Registry ───────────────────────────────┤
        ↓                                          │
Anomaly Registry ──────────────────────────────────┤
        ↓                                          │
Critique Registry ─────────────────────────────────┤
        ↓                                          │
Brenner Method Scorecard ←─────────────────────────┘
        ↓                         (all registries feed)
Research Program Dashboard
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Hypotheses have stable IDs with full metadata (mechanism, anchors, etc.)
- [ ] Hypothesis lifecycle states tracked with transition history
- [ ] Test→Prediction→Hypothesis binding with potency checks
- [ ] Assumption registry with scale_physics enforcement
- [ ] Anomaly registry with hypothesis spawning
- [ ] Critique registry with response workflow
- [ ] 14-criterion scorecard computed per session
- [ ] Program dashboard aggregates all registries

## Non-Goals

- Not predictive analytics (what will be killed next)
- Not automated test execution
- Not replacing human judgment on method quality","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:37:32.539527383-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T20:49:03.980713668-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T20:49:03.980713668-05:00","close_reason":"All 36 child tasks completed. Research Intelligence System now has: Hypothesis Registry, Assumption Registry, Anomaly Registry, Critique Registry, Test Registry, Scorecard, Research Program Dashboard, Artifact Semantic Diff, Cross-Session Linking, Session Replay, and Operator Intervention Audit Trail.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:35:50.697324851-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-juv","title":"EPIC: Session Interface & Excerpt Builder","description":"# Purpose

Enable users to run Brenner Loop research sessions via Agent Mail. This is the 'lab' functionality - where the method becomes executable.

# Pages

1. **/sessions** - List of sessions (active, completed) with status
2. **/sessions/new** - Create new session with excerpt builder
3. **/sessions/[id]** - Session detail view with timeline, artifacts

# Components Needed

- **SessionCard** - Card showing session status, agents, thread ID
- **SessionTimeline** - Visual timeline of messages, agent responses
- **ExcerptBuilder** - Select text from documents, build cited excerpt blocks
- **ArtifactViewer** - Display hypothesis slates, tests, ledgers
- **AgentBadge** - Color-coded badge for Claude/GPT/Gemini
- **StatusIndicator** - Visual indicator for session status

# Excerpt Builder Details

The excerpt builder is critical - it's how users compose the context for their sessions:

1. Navigate to a document, select text
2. Selection shows up in 'excerpt basket' with §-anchor
3. User can reorder, add comments
4. Final excerpt block is formatted for inclusion in kickoff prompt

# Technical Decisions

- Excerpt selections stored in client state (localStorage for persistence)
- Session list fetches from Agent Mail API
- Session detail polls for updates or uses WebSocket
- Artifacts rendered as structured markdown

# Lab Mode Gating

Session creation should require auth (lab mode). Public visitors can view existing sessions but not create new ones.

# Depends On

- Design System Foundation
- Corpus Browser (for excerpt selection in documents)
- Landing Page (for navigation)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:12:14.885500044-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:17.966380606-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:17.966380606-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.2 (Excerpt builder) + brenner_bot-5so.4.3 (Sessions UI) + brenner_bot-5so.4.4 (Lab gating)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-juv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1qx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:12:25.471699138-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-juv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-e00","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:12:25.531616279-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
